title: ranunculus infestus
intro: Ranunculus infestus, also known as the common buttercup, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant contains ranunculin, a toxic compound with medicinal properties, which has been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Ranunculus infestus include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus infestus are typically made into a tincture or ointment, which can be applied topically to the affected area.
